instructions: नीचे दिए गए Hindi sentences को English में translate करें। 'Wanted' के past tense structure को ध्यान से use करें।

questions.hindi: मैं पानी पीना चाहता था।
questions.english: I wanted to drink water.
alternatives: i wanted to drink water.
questions.explanation: 'Wanted' is past tense of 'want'. All subjects use the same form: I wanted.

questions.hindi: वह घर जाना चाहता था।
questions.english: He wanted to go home.
alternatives: he wanted to go home.
questions.explanation: 'He wanted to go' — 'to' is necessary between 'wanted' and the verb.

questions.hindi: वह डॉक्टर बनना चाहती थी।
questions.english: She wanted to become a doctor.
alternatives: she wanted to become a doctor.
questions.explanation: 'Become' is the base form. She wanted to become = वह बनना चाहती थी।

questions.hindi: हम बाहर जाना चाहते थे।
questions.english: We wanted to go outside.
alternatives: we wanted to go out.
we wanted to go outdoors.
questions.explanation: 'Wanted' is same for We. 'Outside/out/outdoors' are all acceptable.

questions.hindi: वे खाना खाना चाहते थे।
questions.english: They wanted to eat food.
alternatives: they wanted to eat.
questions.explanation: 'They wanted to eat' — standard structure.

questions.hindi: बच्चा आइसक्रीम चाहता था।
questions.english: The child wanted ice cream.
alternatives: the child wanted ice-cream.
questions.explanation: 'Ice cream' is a noun here, so no 'to + verb'. The child wanted ice cream.

questions.hindi: मुझे आराम चाहिए था।
questions.english: I wanted rest.
alternatives: i wanted some rest.
i wanted a rest.
questions.explanation: 'Rest' is a noun. No 'to + verb' needed.

questions.hindi: उसे एक नई किताब चाहिए थी।
questions.english: She wanted a new book.
alternatives: she wanted a new book.
questions.explanation: 'A new book' is a noun phrase. No verb needed after 'wanted'.

questions.hindi: मैं English सीखना चाहता था।
questions.english: I wanted to learn English.
alternatives: i wanted to learn english.
questions.explanation: 'Wanted to learn' — very common structure for skill acquisition desire.

questions.hindi: वह अपना business शुरू करना चाहता था।
questions.english: He wanted to start his own business.
alternatives: he wanted to start his own business.
questions.explanation: 'Start his own business' — 'his own' shows possession.

questions.hindi: आप कुछ कहना चाहते थे।
questions.english: You wanted to say something.
alternatives: you wanted to say something.
questions.explanation: 'You wanted' — same form. 'Something' is the object.

questions.hindi: वे शांति चाहते थे।
questions.english: They wanted peace.
alternatives: they wanted peace.
questions.explanation: 'Peace' is a noun. Direct object after 'wanted'.

questions.hindi: मुझे तुम्हारी मदद चाहिए थी।
questions.english: I wanted your help.
alternatives: i wanted your help.
i needed your help.
questions.explanation: 'Your help' = possessive noun phrase.

questions.hindi: वह promotion चाहता था।
questions.english: He wanted a promotion.
alternatives: he wanted promotion.
questions.explanation: 'A promotion' — article + noun. Both 'a promotion' and 'promotion' are acceptable.

questions.hindi: हमें और जानकारी चाहिए थी।
questions.english: We wanted more information.
alternatives: we wanted more information.
questions.explanation: 'More information' = quantifier + noun. No article needed for uncountable nouns.

questions.hindi: मैं वहाँ नहीं जाना चाहता था।
questions.english: I did not want to go there.
alternatives: i didn't want to go there.
i did not want to go there.
questions.hint: Subject + did not want + to + verb
questions.explanation: Negative: 'did not want' (NOT 'did not wanted').
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + to + verb

questions.hindi: उसे वह किताब नहीं चाहिए थी।
questions.english: She did not want that book.
alternatives: she didn't want that book.
she did not want that book.
questions.explanation: Negative with noun object: 'did not want' + noun.

questions.hindi: हम उस meeting में नहीं जाना चाहते थे।
questions.english: We did not want to go to that meeting.
alternatives: we didn't want to go to that meeting.
questions.hint: We + did not want + to + go
questions.explanation: 'Did not want to go' = नहीं जाना चाहते थे।
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + to + verb

questions.hindi: वह उस offer को accept नहीं करना चाहता था।
questions.english: He did not want to accept that offer.
alternatives: he didn't want to accept that offer.
questions.hint: He + did not want + to + accept
questions.explanation: 'Accept' stays in base form after 'want to'.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + to + verb

questions.hindi: मुझे कोई गलती नहीं चाहिए थी।
questions.english: I did not want any mistake.
alternatives: i didn't want any mistake.
i did not want any mistakes.
questions.hint: I + did not want + any + noun
questions.explanation: 'Any mistake' is used in negative sentences. Also acceptable: 'any mistakes'.

questions.hindi: उसने माफ़ी नहीं माँगनी चाही।
questions.english: She did not want to apologize.
alternatives: she didn't want to apologize.
questions.hint: She + did not want + to + verb
questions.explanation: 'Apologize' is the base verb. Negative past with 'did not want'.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + to + verb

questions.hindi: बच्चे पार्क नहीं जाना चाहते थे।
questions.english: The children did not want to go to the park.
alternatives: the children didn't want to go to the park.
questions.hint: The children + did not want + to + go
questions.explanation: 'The children' = plural subject. 'Did not want' is same for all.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + to + verb

questions.hindi: Team को और training नहीं चाहिए थी।
questions.english: The team did not want more training.
alternatives: the team didn't want more training.
questions.hint: Subject + did not want + noun phrase
questions.explanation: 'More training' is a noun phrase used as object.

questions.hindi: मैं उससे उस विषय पर बात नहीं करना चाहता था।
questions.english: I did not want to talk to him about that topic.
alternatives: i didn't want to talk to him about that topic.
questions.hint: I + did not want + to + talk + to + him
questions.explanation: 'Talk to him' = preposition phrase. Long negative sentence.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + to + verb

questions.hindi: उसे extra time नहीं चाहिए था।
questions.english: She did not want extra time.
alternatives: she didn't want extra time.
questions.hint: She + did not want + noun phrase
questions.explanation: 'Extra time' = adjective + noun. No article needed.

questions.hindi: वे उस project में शामिल नहीं होना चाहते थे।
questions.english: They did not want to join that project.
alternatives: they didn't want to join that project.
questions.hint: They + did not want + to + join
questions.explanation: 'Join' = base verb. Past negative with 'did not want'.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + to + verb

questions.hindi: क्या तुम चाय पीना चाहते थे?
questions.english: Did you want to drink tea?
alternatives: did you want tea?
questions.explanation: Question form: 'Did' at start, 'want' in base form (NOT 'wanted').

questions.hindi: क्या उसे मदद चाहिए थी?
questions.english: Did she want any help?
alternatives: did she want help?
did she want some help?
questions.explanation: Questions with noun object. 'Any/some/help' all acceptable.

questions.hindi: क्या वह वहाँ जाना चाहता था?
questions.english: Did he want to go there?
alternatives: did he want to go there?
questions.explanation: 'Did he want' — NOT 'did he wanted'. Common mistake to avoid.

questions.hindi: क्या आप report submit करना चाहते थे?
questions.english: Did you want to submit the report?
alternatives: did you want to submit the report?
questions.explanation: Professional question using 'Did you want to + verb'.

questions.hindi: क्या वे उस party में जाना चाहते थे?
questions.english: Did they want to go to that party?
alternatives: did they want to go to the party?
questions.explanation: 'They' uses same 'want' form. 'That/the' both acceptable.

questions.hindi: क्या manager ने meeting जल्दी खत्म करनी चाही?
questions.english: Did the manager want to end the meeting early?
alternatives: did the manager want to finish the meeting early?
questions.hint: Did + the manager + want + to + end?
questions.explanation: 'End/finish' both work. Article 'the' needed for manager.

questions.hindi: क्या client को पूरी जानकारी चाहिए थी?
questions.english: Did the client want complete information?
alternatives: did the client want full information?
questions.hint: Did + the client + want + noun phrase?
questions.explanation: 'Complete/full' both acceptable as adjective.

questions.hindi: मैं इस मामले पर आपसे बात करना चाहता था।
questions.english: I wanted to discuss this matter with you.
alternatives: i wanted to talk about this matter with you.
questions.explanation: 'Discuss' = polite professional verb. Also 'talk about' is acceptable.

questions.hindi: वह हमेशा से pilot बनना चाहता था।
questions.english: He always wanted to become a pilot.
alternatives: he had always wanted to become a pilot.
questions.explanation: 'Always wanted' — 'always' placed before 'wanted' for emphasis.

questions.hindi: मैं बस शुक्रिया कहना चाहता था।
questions.english: I just wanted to say thank you.
alternatives: i just wanted to thank you.
questions.explanation: 'Just wanted to say' is a very polite spoken/written expression.

questions.hindi: उसने कहा कि वह मुझसे मिलना चाहती थी।
questions.english: She said she wanted to meet me.
alternatives: she said that she wanted to meet me.
questions.hint: Indirect speech: She said she wanted to...
questions.explanation: Indirect speech: 'wants' becomes 'wanted' in reported speech.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + said + (that) + subject + wanted + to + verb

questions.hindi: Client एक detailed proposal चाहता था।
questions.english: The client wanted a detailed proposal.
alternatives: the client wanted a detailed proposal.
questions.explanation: 'A detailed proposal' = article + adjective + noun.

questions.hindi: मैं अपनी पुरानी नौकरी छोड़ना चाहता था।
questions.english: I wanted to leave my old job.
alternatives: i wanted to quit my old job.
questions.explanation: 'Leave/quit' — both acceptable. 'My old job' = possessive + adjective + noun.

questions.hindi: वह एक बेहतर वेतन चाहती थी।
questions.english: She wanted a better salary.
alternatives: she wanted a higher salary.
questions.explanation: 'Better/higher salary' — both adjectives acceptable.

questions.hindi: हम मिलकर इस problem को solve करना चाहते थे।
questions.english: We wanted to solve this problem together.
alternatives: we wanted to solve the problem together.
questions.explanation: 'Together' adds emphasis on collaboration.

questions.hindi: मैं तुम्हें call करना चाहता था पर busy था।
questions.english: I wanted to call you but I was busy.
alternatives: i wanted to call you but was busy.
questions.hint: I + wanted + to + call + but + I + was + busy
questions.explanation: 'But' connects two past situations. 'Wanted to but couldn't' = unfulfilled desire.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + wanted + to + verb + but + past situation

questions.hindi: वह उस दिन घर जल्दी जाना चाहती थी।
questions.english: She wanted to go home early that day.
alternatives: she wanted to go home early on that day.
questions.hint: She + wanted + to + go + home + early
questions.explanation: 'That day' indicates a specific past day.

questions.hindi: बचपन में मैं astronaut बनना चाहता था।
questions.english: As a child, I wanted to become an astronaut.
alternatives: in childhood, i wanted to become an astronaut.
questions.hint: As a child + I + wanted + to + become
questions.explanation: 'As a child' = time phrase at beginning. 'An astronaut' uses 'an' before vowel sound.
questions.grammarRule: Time phrase + subject + wanted + to + verb

questions.hindi: मैं इस field में हमेशा से काम करना चाहता था, इसीलिए मैंने यह course किया।
questions.english: I always wanted to work in this field, which is why I did this course.
alternatives: i always wanted to work in this field, so i took this course.
questions.hint: I + always + wanted + to + work + complex sentence
questions.explanation: Complex sentence: 'wanted to' + reason clause. 'Which is why' = formal connector.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + always + wanted + to + verb + reason clause

questions.hindi: यदि मुझे पता होता, तो मैं वहाँ जाना नहीं चाहता था।
questions.english: If I had known, I would not have wanted to go there.
alternatives: if i had known, i wouldn't have wanted to go there.
questions.hint: Conditional: If I had known... would not have wanted
questions.explanation: Third conditional: 'If had known + would not have wanted to'. This is advanced.
questions.grammarRule: If + past perfect + would not have wanted + to + verb

questions.hindi: उन्होंने कहा कि वे project को जल्दी खत्म करना चाहते थे, लेकिन resources नहीं थे।
questions.english: They said they wanted to finish the project quickly, but they did not have resources.
alternatives: they said they wanted to complete the project quickly, but they lacked resources.
questions.hint: They said they wanted + but + past problem
questions.explanation: Indirect speech + unfulfilled desire. 'Did not have' = past negative of 'have'.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + said + wanted + to + verb + but + obstacle

questions.hindi: Manager ने पूछा कि क्या हम overtime करना चाहते थे।
questions.english: The manager asked if we wanted to do overtime.
alternatives: the manager asked whether we wanted to do overtime.
questions.hint: Indirect question: asked if we wanted to...
questions.explanation: Indirect question: 'if/whether we wanted to' — embedded question structure.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + asked + if/whether + subject + wanted + to + verb

questions.hindi: हम सब चाहते थे कि company नए markets में जाए।
questions.english: We all wanted the company to enter new markets.
alternatives: we all wanted the company to expand into new markets.
questions.hint: We + wanted + the company + to + enter
questions.explanation: 'Wanted someone to do something' = Subject + wanted + object + to + verb. Advanced pattern.

questions.hindi: उसे नहीं पता था कि वह क्या चाहती थी।
questions.english: She did not know what she wanted.
alternatives: she didn't know what she wanted.
questions.hint: She + did not know + what + she + wanted
questions.explanation: Embedded question: 'what she wanted' — no 'to' because 'wanted' is the main verb here.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not know + what + subject + wanted

questions.hindi: क्या आप सच में उस नौकरी को छोड़ना चाहते थे, या सिर्फ break चाहिए था?
questions.english: Did you really want to quit that job, or did you just want a break?
alternatives: did you really want to leave that job, or did you just need a break?
questions.hint: Did + you + really + want + to + quit ... or + did + you + just + want?
questions.explanation: Alternative question with 'or'. Two separate 'did you want' structures.
questions.grammarRule: Did + subject + want + to + verb + or + did + subject + want + noun?

questions.hindi: वे एक ऐसी company में काम करना चाहते थे जहाँ उनकी बात सुनी जाए।
questions.english: They wanted to work in a company where their voice was heard.
alternatives: they wanted to work in a company where they were heard.
questions.hint: They + wanted + to + work + in + a company + where + clause
questions.explanation: Relative clause with 'where'. 'Voice was heard' = passive voice in relative clause.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + wanted + to + verb + relative clause

questions.hindi: मैं gym जाना चाहता था लेकिन बारिश हो गई।
questions.english: I wanted to go to the gym but it rained.
alternatives: i wanted to go to the gym but it was raining.
questions.hint: I + wanted + to + go + but + obstacle
questions.explanation: 'But' + past event showing why desire was unfulfilled.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + wanted + to + verb + but + past situation

questions.hindi: क्या तुम gym जाना चाहते थे?
questions.english: Did you want to go to the gym?
alternatives: did you want to go to the gym?
questions.explanation: Simple past question. 'Gym' usually used with 'the'.

questions.hindi: उसने उस दिन कुछ भी नहीं खाना चाहा।
questions.english: She did not want to eat anything that day.
alternatives: she didn't want to eat anything that day.
questions.hint: She + did not want + to + eat + anything
questions.explanation: 'Anything' used in negative sentences. 'That day' = specific past time.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + to + verb

questions.hindi: वह हमेशा विदेश जाना चाहता था।
questions.english: He always wanted to go abroad.
alternatives: he always wanted to travel abroad.
questions.hint: He + always + wanted + to + go abroad
questions.explanation: 'Abroad' = to a foreign country. 'Always' shows lifelong desire.

questions.hindi: मुझे एक नई notebook चाहिए थी।
questions.english: I wanted a new notebook.
alternatives: i wanted a new notebook.
questions.explanation: 'A new notebook' = article + adjective + noun. Simple noun object.

questions.hindi: हम उस restaurant में खाना चाहते थे।
questions.english: We wanted to eat at that restaurant.
alternatives: we wanted to dine at that restaurant.
questions.hint: We + wanted + to + eat + at + that restaurant
questions.explanation: 'Eat at / dine at + restaurant' = standard phrasing.

questions.hindi: क्या उसे coffee चाहिए थी?
questions.english: Did she want coffee?
alternatives: did she want a coffee?
did she want some coffee?
questions.explanation: 'Coffee' = uncountable. 'A coffee/some coffee' also acceptable in informal context.

questions.hindi: मैं यह बात पहले ही कहना चाहता था।
questions.english: I wanted to say this earlier.
alternatives: i wanted to tell you this earlier.
questions.hint: I + wanted + to + say/tell + this + earlier
questions.explanation: 'Earlier' = comparative adverb for time.

questions.hindi: क्या आप salary बढ़ाने की बात करना चाहते थे?
questions.english: Did you want to discuss a salary raise?
alternatives: did you want to talk about a salary increase?
questions.explanation: 'Discuss/talk about' = both acceptable. 'Salary raise/increase' = both natural.

questions.hindi: वे एक बेहतर भविष्य चाहते थे।
questions.english: They wanted a better future.
alternatives: they wanted a brighter future.
questions.explanation: 'Better/brighter future' — adjective + abstract noun.

questions.hindi: मैं interview में अच्छा करना चाहता था।
questions.english: I wanted to do well in the interview.
alternatives: i wanted to perform well in the interview.
questions.hint: I + wanted + to + do well + in the interview
questions.explanation: 'Do well' = perform effectively. 'In the interview' = location.

questions.hindi: उसे खुद को prove करना था।
questions.english: She wanted to prove herself.
alternatives: she wanted to prove herself.
questions.explanation: 'Prove herself' = reflexive pronoun showing self-motivation.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + wanted + to + verb + reflexive pronoun

questions.hindi: क्या team leader ने सभी को training देना चाही?
questions.english: Did the team leader want to train everyone?
alternatives: did the team leader want to give training to everyone?
questions.hint: Did + the team leader + want + to + train?
questions.explanation: 'Train everyone' = direct object. 'Give training to' also acceptable.

questions.hindi: मुझे इस job में join होना था।
questions.english: I wanted to join this job.
alternatives: i wanted to join this company.
i wanted to take this job.
questions.explanation: 'Join this job/company/organization' = all are natural.

questions.hindi: हम सफल होना चाहते थे।
questions.english: We wanted to succeed.
alternatives: we wanted to be successful.
we wanted success.
questions.explanation: 'Succeed' = base verb. 'Be successful' = adjective form. 'Success' = noun form.

questions.hindi: क्या वह अपना खुद का काम शुरू करना चाहता था?
questions.english: Did he want to start his own business?
alternatives: did he want to start his own work?
did he want to start his own venture?
questions.explanation: 'Business/work/venture' — all acceptable for 'काम'.

questions.hindi: मैं उस दिन जल्दी उठना चाहता था।
questions.english: I wanted to wake up early that day.
alternatives: i wanted to get up early that day.
questions.hint: I + wanted + to + wake up + early
questions.explanation: 'Wake up / get up' — both common phrasal verbs for rising from sleep.

questions.hindi: वह class में first आना चाहती थी।
questions.english: She wanted to come first in the class.
alternatives: she wanted to rank first in class.
questions.explanation: 'Come first' = rank 1st. 'In the class' = location context.

questions.hindi: हम इस project में एक अच्छी team बनाना चाहते थे।
questions.english: We wanted to build a good team for this project.
alternatives: we wanted to form a good team for this project.
questions.hint: We + wanted + to + build + a good team
questions.explanation: 'Build/form a team' = both natural professional expressions.

questions.hindi: क्या तुम उस film को देखना चाहते थे?
questions.english: Did you want to watch that film?
alternatives: did you want to see that film?
did you want to watch that movie?
questions.explanation: 'Watch/see a film/movie' = all are correct.

questions.hindi: उसे office में transfer नहीं चाहिए था।
questions.english: He did not want a transfer in the office.
alternatives: he didn't want to be transferred.
he didn't want a transfer.
questions.explanation: 'A transfer' = noun. Also: 'didn't want to be transferred' = passive form.

questions.hindi: मुझे उससे कोई मतलब नहीं था।
questions.english: I did not want anything to do with him.
alternatives: i didn't want anything to do with him.
questions.hint: I + did not want + anything + to do with + him
questions.explanation: 'Didn't want anything to do with' = common idiom showing disinterest.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + anything to do with + object

questions.hindi: वह इस बारे में किसी को नहीं बताना चाहता था।
questions.english: He did not want to tell anyone about this.
alternatives: he didn't want anyone to know about this.
questions.hint: He + did not want + to + tell + anyone
questions.explanation: 'Tell anyone about this' = informing someone. 'Anyone' in negative.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + to + verb

questions.hindi: क्या वे company छोड़ना चाहते थे?
questions.english: Did they want to leave the company?
alternatives: did they want to quit the company?
did they want to resign?
questions.explanation: 'Leave/quit/resign' = all acceptable in professional context.

questions.hindi: मैं उस trip पर जाना चाहता था लेकिन पैसे नहीं थे।
questions.english: I wanted to go on that trip but I did not have money.
alternatives: i wanted to go on that trip but i didn't have money.
questions.hint: I + wanted + to + go + but + obstacle
questions.explanation: 'Did not have money' = past negative of 'have'.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + wanted + to + verb + but + obstacle

questions.hindi: उसे इस काम में किसी की मदद नहीं चाहिए थी।
questions.english: She did not want anyone's help with this work.
alternatives: she didn't want any help with this work.
questions.hint: She + did not want + anyone's help
questions.explanation: 'Anyone's help' = possessive. 'Any help' also correct.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + possessive + noun

questions.hindi: हम एक नए घर में शिफ्ट होना चाहते थे।
questions.english: We wanted to move to a new house.
alternatives: we wanted to shift to a new house.
questions.hint: We + wanted + to + move/shift + to + a new house
questions.explanation: 'Move to/shift to' = both acceptable in Indian English context.

questions.hindi: क्या आप इस event में participate करना चाहते थे?
questions.english: Did you want to participate in this event?
alternatives: did you want to take part in this event?
questions.explanation: 'Participate in / take part in' = same meaning.

questions.hindi: वह अपने parents को खुश देखना चाहता था।
questions.english: He wanted to see his parents happy.
alternatives: he wanted his parents to be happy.
questions.hint: He + wanted + to + see + noun + adjective
questions.explanation: 'Wanted to see someone + adjective' = observing desired state. 'Wanted someone to be' = alternative.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + wanted + to + verb + object + adjective

questions.hindi: मैं interview से पहले और practice करना चाहता था।
questions.english: I wanted to practice more before the interview.
alternatives: i wanted more practice before the interview.
questions.hint: I + wanted + to + practice + more + before
questions.explanation: 'Practice more' = verb form. 'More practice' = noun form. Both work.

questions.hindi: क्या उन्हें उस time पर कुछ खाना चाहिए था?
questions.english: Did they want to eat something at that time?
alternatives: did they want something to eat at that time?
questions.hint: Did + they + want + to + eat + something?
questions.explanation: 'Want to eat something' or 'want something to eat' — both natural.

questions.hindi: वह इस company में contribute करना चाहती थी।
questions.english: She wanted to contribute to this company.
alternatives: she wanted to contribute to the company.
questions.explanation: 'Contribute to' = phrasal verb with preposition. Always use 'to' after 'contribute'.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + wanted + to + contribute + to + noun

questions.hindi: मुझे उस दिन एक अच्छा मौका मिलना चाहिए था।
questions.english: I wanted a good opportunity that day.
alternatives: i wanted to get a good opportunity that day.
questions.explanation: 'A good opportunity' = article + adjective + noun object.

questions.hindi: क्या वह उस project में lead करना चाहता था?
questions.english: Did he want to lead that project?
alternatives: did he want to head that project?
questions.explanation: 'Lead/head a project' = both correct professional expressions.

questions.hindi: हम एक ऐसा solution चाहते थे जो सबके लिए काम करे।
questions.english: We wanted a solution that worked for everyone.
alternatives: we wanted a solution that would work for everyone.
questions.explanation: Relative clause: 'that worked for everyone'. 'Worked/would work' both acceptable.

questions.hindi: मुझे उस बात की चिंता नहीं करनी थी।
questions.english: I did not want to worry about that.
alternatives: i didn't want to worry about it.
questions.hint: I + did not want + to + worry + about
questions.explanation: 'Worry about' = phrasal verb. 'That/it' both acceptable.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + did not want + to + verb + about

questions.hindi: वह एक ऐसी ज़िंदगी जीना चाहती थी जिसमें कोई पछतावा न हो।
questions.english: She wanted to live a life with no regrets.
alternatives: she wanted to live a life without regrets.
questions.hint: She + wanted + to + live + a life + with no regrets
questions.explanation: 'With no regrets / without regrets' = both natural.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + wanted + to + verb + noun phrase

questions.hindi: क्या HR ने employees को नई policy बतानी चाही?
questions.english: Did HR want to inform employees about the new policy?
alternatives: did HR want to tell the employees about the new policy?
questions.explanation: 'Inform/tell about' = both correct. HR is treated as singular.

questions.hindi: मैं उस दिन घर जल्दी जाना चाहता था क्योंकि मेरे पिताजी आए थे।
questions.english: I wanted to go home early that day because my father had come.
alternatives: i wanted to go home early that day as my father had arrived.
questions.hint: I + wanted + to + go home early + because + past perfect
questions.explanation: 'Because/as' = reason connector. 'Had come/had arrived' = past perfect.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + wanted + to + verb + reason clause

questions.hindi: वे एक नई technology adopt करना चाहते थे।
questions.english: They wanted to adopt a new technology.
alternatives: they wanted to implement a new technology.
questions.explanation: 'Adopt/implement' = both professional verbs for technology.

questions.hindi: मैं उस conference में present होना चाहता था।
questions.english: I wanted to be present at that conference.
alternatives: i wanted to attend that conference.
questions.hint: I + wanted + to + be present at / attend
questions.explanation: 'Be present at' or 'attend' = both correct formal expressions.
